perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Agents' services to be rendered in connection with the Offering, the Corporation shall pay to the Agents a cash fee (the "Agents' Fee") equal to 7.0% of the gross proceeds of the Offering. As additional consideration, the Corporation shall issue to the Agents that number of compensation options (the "Compensation Options") equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. The Compensation Options will be exercisable for a period of 24 months at the Offering Price. Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Agents' services to be rendered in connection with the Offering, the Corporation shall pay to the Agents a cash fee (the "Brokerage Fee") equal to 7.0% of the gross proceeds of the Offering. As additional consideration, the Corporation may, at its discretion, issue to the Agents that number of compensation options (the "Compensation Options") equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. The Compensation Options will be exercisable for a period of 24 months at the Offering Price. Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Agents' Fee' to 'Brokerage Fee' and replacing 'shall pay' to 'may, at its discretion, issue' creates uncertainty regarding the Corporation's obligation to pay the Agents' fee. While the original text explicitly states the Corporation 'shall pay', the modified version suggests it's discretionary. This contradicts later sections that assume a mandatory fee payment. It causes confusion and legal challenges during enforcement as the new statement implies that the Corporation has full discretion over whether or not to issue the compensation options.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for the Agents' services hereunder, the Corporation will pay to the Agents the Agents' Fee and the Corporation shall issue to the Agents that number of Compensation Options equal to 3.5% of the number of Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering. Unless otherwise stated herein, the Offered Shares shall also refer to the Compensation Options. The Agents' Fee shall be apportioned among the Agents as follows: Dundee Securities Ltd. 40% Kes 7 Capital Inc. 30% Bloom Burton & Co. Ltd. 30% 100%
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Agents' Fee' to 'Brokerage Fee' and replaces 'shall pay' to 'may, at its discretion, issue.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, 'the Corporation will pay to the Agents the Agents' Fee' and 'the Corporation shall issue to the Agents.' The original contract mandates payment and issuance of compensation options, while the modified version makes the issuance of compensation options discretionary, creating a conflict about the Corporation's obligations and introducing uncertainty regarding the mandatory nature of the agents' fee.

perturbation.original_text: Agents agree to obtain from each Subscriber executed Subscription Agreements (including the execution of applicable Schedules to such Subscription Agreements) and deliver such Subscription Agreements (including applicable Schedules) to the Corporation at or prior to the Closing Time. In addition, the Agents agree to obtain from each Subscriber such forms and other documents as may be required by the Securities Commissions and by the Corporation's registrar and provided by the Corporation to the Agents for delivery under this Agreement. The Corporation and the Agents shall agree on the allocation of the Offered Shares amongst the Subscribers.
perturbation.changed_text: Agents agree to use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain from each Subscriber executed Subscription Agreements (including the execution of applicable Schedules to such Subscription Agreements) and use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver such Subscription Agreements (including applicable Schedules) to the Corporation at or prior to the Closing Time. In addition, the Agents may attempt to obtain from each Subscriber such forms and other documents as may be required by the Securities Commissions and by the Corporation's registrar and provided by the Corporation to the Agents for delivery under this Agreement. The Corporation and the Agents may attempt to agree on the allocation of the Offered Shares amongst the Subscribers.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agree to obtain and deliver' to 'use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain and use commercially reasonable efforts to deliver' weakens the Agents' obligation, introducing ambiguity. Also changing 'agree to obtain' to 'may attempt to obtain' and 'shall agree' to 'may attempt to agree' undermines the mandatory nature of the agreements on Subscribers forms and allocation. It creates uncertainty whether the Subscription Agreements can be obtained or can't. The new language suggests a weaker commitment, leaving room for interpretation and potential disputes. This directly contradicts the conditions of the closing where such agreements are required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At the Closing Time, the Corporation shall deliver to the Agents: (a) the opinions, certificates and agreements referred to in Section 9 and all other documents required to be provided by the Corporation to the Agents pursuant to this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements; (b) except for any Offered Shares offered or sold in the United States which shall be represented by individual definitive share certificates, one or more definitive share certificate(s)/and or book-entry only securities, duly registered as the Lead Agent may direct the Corporation; (c) the Corporation's receipt for payment by the Agents of an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering; and (d) such further documentation as may be contemplated by this Agreement or as Agents' Counsel or the applicable regulatory authorities may reasonably require; against delivery by the Agents of: (e) all duly completed Subscription Agreements tendered by the Subscribers for the Offered Shares being issued and sold and, where applicable, all completed forms, schedules and certificates contemplated by such Subscription Agreements; (f) a wire transfer of immediately available funds in an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering, less an amount equal to the Agents' Fee and the costs and expenses of the Agent provided for in Section 14; and (g) the Agents' receipt for the Agents' Fee and the definitive certificates delivered to the Agents in accordance with Section 6(b).
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the agents' obligations to 'use commercially reasonable efforts' and 'may attempt to'. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires 'all duly completed Subscription Agreements tendered by the Subscribers' at the Closing Time. The change introduces uncertainty as to whether the agreements will actually be obtained and delivered, creating a direct conflict with the requirement for 'all duly completed Subscription Agreements' being delivered at closing, thus leading to a potential breach of contract if the agents only use 'commercially reasonable efforts' and fail to obtain all agreements.

perturbation.original_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) is duly amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) believes it is amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and believes it is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) believes it has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) believes it has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.explanation: By changing assertions of fact to statements of belief (e.g., 'is duly amalgamated' to 'believes it is amalgamated'), the legal strength of the Corporation's warranties is diminished. The original language represents a guarantee about the corporation's status while the modified language introduces uncertainty. This significantly weakens the Agents' ability to rely on these warranties. The addition of “believes” before key facts introduces ambiguity regarding the veracity of critical details. This contrasts directly with Section 9(b)(iii), where the officers must certify that certain facts are true after having made due enquiry, rather than simply believing them to be true.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (iii) The Agents shall have received at the Closing Time a certificate dated the Closing Date, addressed to the Agents and counsel to the Agents and signed on behalf of the Corporation by the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer or other officers of the Corporation acceptable to the Agents, certifying for and on behalf of the Corporation and without personal liability, after having made due enquiry: (A) the Corporation has complied with and satisfied all terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements on its part to be complied with or satisfied at or prior to the Closing Time; 18 (B) the representations and warranties of the Corporation contained in this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements are true and correct at the Closing Time with the same force and effect as if made at and as of the Closing Time after giving effect to the transactions contemplated by this Agreement; (C) the responses provided by the Corporation at the Due Diligence Session are true and correct and would not be different in any material respect if the Due Diligence Session were held immediately prior to the Closing Time; (D) the Corporation has made and/or obtained, on or prior to the Closing Time, all necessary filings, approvals, consents and acceptances of applicable regulatory authorities and under any applicable agreement or document to which the Corporation is a party or by which it is bound, required for the execution and delivery of this Agreement, the offering and sale of the Offered Shares in the Selling Jurisdictions in Canada and the consummation of the other transactions contemplated hereby (subject to completion of filings with certain regulatory authorities following the Closing Date); (E) no order, ruling or determination having the effect of suspending the sale of or cease trading the Offered Shares or any other securities of the Corporation has been issued by any regulatory authority and is continuing in effect and no proceedings for that purpose have been instituted or are pending or, to the knowledge of such officer of the Corporation, contemplated or threatened under any Applicable Securities Laws or by any other regulatory authority; and (F) such other matters as may be reasonably requested by the Agents or the Agents' Counsel.
perturbation.justification: The original text presents the Corporation's representations as facts, whereas the changed text weakens these to statements of belief. This directly contradicts Section (iii)(B) of the contradicted text, which requires the officers to certify that the representations and warranties of the Corporation are 'true and correct'. Belief is insufficient to fulfill this certification requirement, creating a direct conflict between the changed text and the contradicted text. The modification introduces ambiguity about the veracity of critical details, whereas the contradicted text demands certainty, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
